records.ety: [Icel. skaði; akin to Dan. skade, Sw. skada, AS. sceaða, scaða, foe, injurer, OS. skaðo, D. schade, harm, injury, OHG. scade, G. schade, schaden; cf. Gr. 'askhqh`s unharmed. Cf. Scathe, v.]

defs: Harm; damage; injury; hurt; waste; misfortune.
quotes: But she was somedeal deaf, and that was skathe.
Great mercy, sure, for to enlarge a thrall, Whose freedom shall thee turn to greatest scath.
Wherein Rome hath done you any scath, Let him make treble satisfaction.

records.ety: [Icel. skaða; akin to AS. sceaðan, sceððan, Dan. skade, Sw. skada, D. & G. schaden, OHG. scadōn, Goth. skaþjan.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Scathed (skād or sktht); p. pr. & vb. n. Scathing (skā"ng or skth"-).]

defs: To do harm to; to injure; to damage; to waste; to destroy.
quotes: As when heaven's fire Hath scathed the forest oaks or mountain pines.
Strokes of calamity that scathe and scorch the soul.
